description:
You are a compliance-operations analyst who has been asked to generate a
    plain–text audit trail from two source CSV files that already exist on the
    workstation.

    Existing structure
    ------------------
    1. Raw log directory: /home/user/compliance/raw
       • /home/user/compliance/raw/transactions_20230630.csv  
       • /home/user/compliance/raw/users.csv

    2. Target (output) directory: /home/user/compliance/audit  
       • This directory already exists but is empty.

    Raw file contents
    -----------------
    /home/user/compliance/raw/transactions_20230630.csv  
    (5 columns, comma-separated, 1-line header, UNIX newlines)

        timestamp,transaction_id,user_id,amount,status
        2023-06-30T09:15:44Z,TN001,U1001,250.00,APPROVED
        2023-06-30T10:05:37Z,TN002,U1002,145.50,DECLINED
        2023-06-30T11:22:08Z,TN003,U1003,980.00,APPROVED

    /home/user/compliance/raw/users.csv  
    (2 columns, comma-separated, 1-line header, UNIX newlines)

        user_id,user_name
        U1001,Alice Huang
        U1002,Bob Singh
        U1003,Charlie Garcia

    Your assignment
    ---------------
    1. Produce a new pipe-delimited (“|”) audit file named
       /home/user/compliance/audit/audit_trail_20230630.csv

    2. The audit file MUST contain exactly 6 columns, in the order shown
       below, including the header row:

          timestamp|transaction_id|user_id|user_name|amount|status

       For every data row, copy the values of columns 1–3, 4 and 5 from
       transactions_20230630.csv and insert the matching user_name field from
       users.csv so that each row has:

          a. timestamp  (from transactions column 1)
          b. transaction_id  (from transactions column 2)
          c. user_id  (from transactions column 3)
          d. user_name  (from users column 2)
          e. amount  (from transactions column 4)
          f. status  (from transactions column 5)

       The user_name must correspond to the SAME user_id that appears on the
       row coming from transactions_20230630.csv.

       The resulting file must use exactly one pipe character (“|”) between
       columns, no surrounding quotation marks, and terminate every line with
       a single UNIX newline character.  Do NOT leave any trailing space.

    3. Generate a human-readable log file named
       /home/user/compliance/audit/build_audit_trail.log
       that records, one command per line, the sequence of 5–10 shell
       commands you executed (without their output).  The first line of the
       log must be:

           COMMAND_LOG_START

       and the final line must be:

           COMMAND_LOG_END

       Everything in between must be the literal commands you used, in
       chronological order, exactly as you typed them (no prompt, no
       timestamps, no output).

    4. When you are done, the /home/user/compliance/audit directory should
       contain only the two files mentioned above, with permissions
       -rw-r--r-- (chmod 644) and owned by the current user.

    Tips & constraints
    ------------------
    • Rely only on standard POSIX utilities that are normally available in a
      minimal Linux environment: cut, paste, tr, sed, awk, sort, etc.
    • Do NOT edit the source files; treat them as read-only.
    • Avoid temporary files outside the audit directory; if you create
      intermediate files, delete them before finishing.
    • Your command log must show between 5 and 10 individual commands; use
      compound commands (&&, |) wisely.
    • Remember that the automated test will:
        1. Compare audit_trail_20230630.csv byte-for-byte against the expected
           reference file.
        2. Confirm that build_audit_trail.log matches the required envelope
           (COMMAND_LOG_START…COMMAND_LOG_END) and contains 5–10 command
           lines.
        3. Verify directory contents and permissions.

    Produce both deliverables exactly as requested so the automated verifier
    passes.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (before the agent begins)
    --------------------------------------------------
    Directories:
        /home/user/compliance
        /home/user/compliance/raw
        /home/user/compliance/audit

    Files and contents (UNIX newlines):

    1. /home/user/compliance/raw/transactions_20230630.csv
       timestamp,transaction_id,user_id,amount,status
       2023-06-30T09:15:44Z,TN001,U1001,250.00,APPROVED
       2023-06-30T10:05:37Z,TN002,U1002,145.50,DECLINED
       2023-06-30T11:22:08Z,TN003,U1003,980.00,APPROVED

    2. /home/user/compliance/raw/users.csv
       user_id,user_name
       U1001,Alice Huang
       U1002,Bob Singh
       U1003,Charlie Garcia

    Expected end-state after the agent completes the task
    ------------------------------------------------------
    Directory /home/user/compliance/audit contains EXACTLY:

    A. /home/user/compliance/audit/audit_trail_20230630.csv  
       Expected byte-for-byte content (UNIX newlines, no trailing spaces):

       timestamp|transaction_id|user_id|user_name|amount|status
       2023-06-30T09:15:44Z|TN001|U1001|Alice Huang|250.00|APPROVED
       2023-06-30T10:05:37Z|TN002|U1002|Bob Singh|145.50|DECLINED
       2023-06-30T11:22:08Z|TN003|U1003|Charlie Garcia|980.00|APPROVED

    B. /home/user/compliance/audit/build_audit_trail.log  
       • First line:  COMMAND_LOG_START  
       • Last  line:  COMMAND_LOG_END  
       • Between them: 5 ≤ N ≤ 10 lines, each line the exact command entered
         (the automated grader checks only that the log envelope and line
         count are present, not the actual commands).

    Permissions:
       Both files: -rw-r--r-- (octal 0644) owned by user:user.

    The raw files and their content remain unchanged.
